repo_state.notes: Workspace already had unrelated untracked files under code/option3-overlap/ and findings/artifacts/option-3/.

metrics.key_question: Does same-id container dirty refresh preserve seminaive visibility?
metrics.option_implication: Container refresh needs an equivalent first-class invalidation signal. The native dirty_ids split is oracle evidence for the behavior, not a required implementation shape for a replacement backend.
metrics.performance_summary: Four container dirty-refresh checks passed in 1897907 us total.
limitations: Current binaries do not expose a direct refresh-row counter or row retraction/reinsertion counters.
The unit test confirms the same-id vs changed-id split; the actual refresh-row count is source-inferred from the table refresh path.

verdict: Supported by source and runtime checks; same-id dirty_ids are confirmed and align with the refresh-lane design.
